UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents the owner of the portfolio website, including their alias, bio, and contact information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

alias.description: The prominent alias or name displayed on the portfolio (e.g., 'b.max.dev').

tagline.description: A short, catchy phrase or slogan associated with the portfolio owner.

bio.description: A detailed biographical description of the portfolio owner.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the profile image or avatar of the portfolio owner.

contactEmail.description: Professional email address for contacting the portfolio owner.

resumeUrl.description: URL to a downloadable resume or CV document.

SocialMediaLink.title: SocialMediaLink
SocialMediaLink.description: Represents a link to a social media or professional profile associated with the portfolio owner.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialMediaLink entity.

platform.description: The name of the social media or professional platform (e.g., 'GitHub', 'LinkedIn').

url.description: The URL to the portfolio owner's profile on the specified platform.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity this link belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SocialMediaLink)

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a distinct section or category within the portfolio, used for organizing items like 'Continuous Works' or 'Build Projects'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The display name of the category (e.g., 'Build Projects', 'Skills Learning').

description.description: A brief explanation of what type of portfolio items belong to this category.

displayOrder.description: A numerical value indicating the order in which categories should be displayed in navigation.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a technical skill, technology, or competence demonstrated or acquired by the portfolio owner.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'React', 'Python', 'Data Modeling').

description.description: A brief description or context for the skill, if needed.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon image representing the skill.

PortfolioItem.title: PortfolioItem
PortfolioItem.description: Represents a single project, work entry, skill learned, or hobby showcased in the portfolio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioItem entity.

title.description: The main title of the portfolio item (e.g., 'E-commerce Platform', 'Learning AWS Cloud').

summary.description: A concise, AI-generated summary of the item, suitable for display on item cards.

description.description: A comprehensive, detailed description of the portfolio item for its dedicated view.

thumbnailImageUrl.description: URL to the primary image displayed on the item card.

detailImageUrls.description: An array of URLs for additional images providing more visual context in the detailed view.

externalLinkUrl.description: URL to an external resource related to the item, such as a live demo, repository, or detailed article.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category entity this portfolio item belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N PortfolioItem)

skillIds.description: References to Skill entities associated with this portfolio item, representing technologies used or skills demonstrated. (Relationship: Skill N:N PortfolioItem)

startDate.description: The start date of the project, work, or learning period.

endDate.description: The end date or completion date of the project, work, or learning period.

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating whether this item should be prominently featured on the portfolio.

displayOrder.description: A numerical value indicating the desired display order of the item within its category or featured list.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that owns this portfolio item. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PortfolioItem)

definition.description: Stores the single owner's profile data for the portfolio. The '{ownerUid}' wildcard represents the authenticated user's ID who owns and manages this portfolio. The 'ownerUid' is implicitly denormalized in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: ownerUid
params.description: The unique user ID of the authenticated portfolio owner.

definition.description: Stores external social media and professional links associated with the portfolio owner. The '{ownerUid}' is denormalized in the path for authorization independence. '{socialMediaLinkId}' is the unique identifier for each social media link.

params.name: ownerUid
params.description: The unique user ID of the authenticated portfolio owner.

params.name: socialMediaLinkId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific social media link.

definition.description: Defines categories used for organizing portfolio items (e.g., 'Build Projects', 'Skills Learning'). The '{ownerUid}' is denormalized in the path for authorization independence. '{categoryId}' is the unique identifier for each category.

params.name: ownerUid
params.description: The unique user ID of the authenticated portfolio owner.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific category.

definition.description: Stores technical skills, technologies, or competences demonstrated by the portfolio owner. The '{ownerUid}' is denormalized in the path for authorization independence. '{skillId}' is the unique identifier for each skill.

params.name: ownerUid
params.description: The unique user ID of the authenticated portfolio owner.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific skill.

definition.description: Stores individual portfolio entries such as projects, work experiences, or hobbies. The '{ownerUid}' is denormalized in the path for authorization independence. '{portfolioItemId}' is the unique identifier for each portfolio item. Documents include 'categoryId' and 'skillIds' as references to related entities.

params.name: ownerUid
params.description: The unique user ID of the authenticated portfolio owner.

params.name: portfolioItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific portfolio item.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed around a single-owner portfolio model, prioritizing authorization independence and query efficiency. All portfolio-related data for the owner is logically grouped under a top-level collection `user_profiles/{ownerUid}`. This structure inherently denormalizes the `ownerUid` into the document path for every related entity (SocialMediaLink, Category, Skill, PortfolioItem).

**Authorization Independence:**
By nesting all owner-specific data under `/user_profiles/{ownerUid}/...`, the `ownerUid` is explicitly part of the document's path. This is the primary denormalization strategy for authorization. For example, to manage a `PortfolioItem`, the security rules can directly check `allow write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerUid && request.auth.uid == ownerUid;` (where `ownerUid` is the wildcard in the path). This eliminates the need for expensive and non-atomic `get()` operations to check parent documents for ownership or membership, thus achieving Authorization Independence. The `UserProfile` entity itself also sits at `/user_profiles/{ownerUid}`, making it self-contained for authorization checks.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This structure ensures that security rules do not act as filters, upholding Query-Ability-Predictability. Since all content under `/user_profiles/{ownerUid}` is intended to be publicly readable, rules for read operations can be simply `allow read: if true;` for all subcollections, provided the `ownerUid` in the path is known (which it will be for the public-facing portfolio of 'b.max.dev').

For management (write operations), the rules would enforce `request.auth.uid == ownerUid`. Any `list` query for items within a specific owner's portfolio (e.g., `/user_profiles/{ownerUid}/portfolio_items`) will only return data already secured by the path itself, meaning the query results are predictable and do not require client-side filtering or server-side filtering beyond what the rules explicitly allow. The structural segregation of data belonging to a specific `ownerUid` ensures that `list` queries are inherently secure and efficient, as they target a specific, authorized subtree.
